items.title: US and Iran exchange strikes in Gulf in latest test of ceasefire
items.summary: The US military says it struck Iranian drones and radar sites. Tehran responds by targeting US bases in Kuwait and Bahrain.

items.title: Putin says there is 'no point' meeting Zelensky over ending Ukraine war
items.summary: The Russian president's refusal comes after his Ukrainian counterpart wrote an open letter calling for face-to-face talks.

items.title: Rumours and speculation as fans forecast date of Taylor Swift's wedding
items.summary: Swifties worldwide are looking for clues about the superstar's nuptials to American football star Travis Kelce.

items.title: Armenia braces for election as Russia piles pressure on pro-West government
items.summary: Incumbent Prime Minister Nikol Pashinyan is seeking a third term despite falling domestic support.

items.title: Iran's football team granted visas to enter US for World Cup, officials say
items.summary: The approval comes just 10 days before the Iranian squad's opening match against New Zealand in Los Angeles.

items.title: Canada bans Texas cattle over flesh-eating screwworm outbreak in US
items.summary: The ban came as a second infected calf was identified in Texas, and the US state declaring a state of disaster over the outbreak.

items.title: Tributes to Buffy and Ted Lasso star Anthony Head after death aged 72
items.summary: The British actor, who also appeared in Merlin and Little Britain, died of complications from pneumonia.

items.title: Palestinian baby killed by Israeli gunfire in West Bank, health ministry says
items.summary: The Israeli military says Friday's "incident is under review", expressing "deep sorrow for any harm caused".

items.title: Nearly 50 people die of thirst in Sahara desert after lorry breaks down
items.summary: Only two survived after trekking more than 50km (30 miles) across the desert to alert the authorities.

items.title: US stocks slump as fears over Big Tech shake Wall Street
items.summary: The Nasdaq saw its biggest daily fall since early 2025.

items.title: Ukraine strikes cargo ships and admits Romania drone blast
items.summary: Ukraine says it struck five ships carrying illegal cargo in the Sea of Azov and in coastal waters of Russian-occupied territories.

items.title: Astronauts return to ISS after sheltering during air leak repair attempt
items.summary: Russian attempt to repair tunnel area sparks safe-haven procedure for five other astronauts onboard.

items.title: Norway's crown princess on lung transplant waiting list, palace says
items.summary: Mette-Marit's condition, which stiffens the lungs, making it hard to breathe, has deteriorated, the royal household says.

items.title: I wanted to quit Eurovision twice - then won it, says Bangaranga singer Dara
items.summary: The Bulgarian singer says her historic victory in Vienna came close to never happening.

items.title: Fury in France after child murder suspect's criminal record released
items.summary: A man suspected of murdering an 11-year-old girl had previously been identified as a potential child molester, records show.

items.title: US actor James Handy stabbed to death, with girlfriend's son arrested
items.summary: Handy, 81, is known for his roles in films including Jumanji and Top Gun: Maverick.

items.title: Three jailed over heist of ancient golden helmet from Dutch museum
items.summary: The men are sentenced to 47 months each for stealing the 2,500-year-old Romanian Coțofenești helmet.

items.title: Everest guide survived six-day ordeal by eating chocolate and 'chewing ice'
items.summary: Dawa Sherpa was spotted alive by a cleaning crew as he slid slowly down the world's tallest mountain and spoke to the BBC from hospital.

items.title: Ex-wife of Dubai ruler's nephew in custody, prosecutors say
items.summary: Zeynab Javadli had not been in contact with any of her friends since Tuesday.

items.title: US journalist pleads guilty to working as China's agent
items.summary: Thomas Weir Pauken II, 50, knowingly worked with people employed by the Chinese government for years.

items.title: Xi Jinping to meet Kim Jong Un in rare visit to North Korea
items.summary: It is taking place weeks after Xi met the leaders of the US and Russia - two countries that loom large over North Korea's foreign policy.

items.title: Hospitality jobs boom as US prepares for World Cup
items.summary: It is the third month in a row US jobs figures have beaten expectations.

items.title: Mangrove forests are healing after decades of human destruction
items.summary: Swampy mangrove forests are staging a surprise comeback - which is good news for coastal communities and the climate.

items.title: China cracks down on soft porn, violence and materialism in viral micro dramas
items.summary: Micro dramas have surged in popularity, but drawn criticism for often sensationalist content.

items.title: Singer Fally Ipupa's pride at being given major DR Congo honour
items.summary: He follows the late great Papa Wemba in becoming a knight of the National Order of the Leopard.

items.title: Hezbollah rejects renewed ceasefire agreed by Israel and Lebanon
items.summary: The United States announced the ceasefire agreement on Wednesday night following a fresh round of talks.

items.title: US plans to fight flesh-eating screwworm outbreak with flies and dogs
items.summary: The insect, which has been found in America for the first time since 1966, is a growing concern in the southern US.

items.title: The cash-in-the-sofa saga that just won't go away for South Africa's president
items.summary: A committee of MPs has been formed to look at the case against Cyril Ramaphosa, but he may survive a vote.

items.title: From Nescafe to Ted Lasso: Anthony Head's life in pictures
items.summary: Anthony Head, who first came to public attention for his role in 1980s Nescafe ads, has died at the age of 72.

items.title: A sari for Mars: Outfit worn by Indian 'rocket woman' at US museum
items.summary: Isro scientist Nandini Harinath wore the sari on the "single most critical day" of India's Mars mission.

items.title: Giant banquets rile radical left in France
items.summary: The feasting phenomenon run by Le Canon Français has become a hot political issue.

items.title: Fall in official Ebola numbers appears to be good news but it's not that simple
items.summary: There are now 380 confirmed cases of Ebola in DR Congo, far lower than initial estimates of suspected cases, writes Fergus Walsh.

items.title: Is there an AI stock market bubble, and is it ready to burst?
items.summary: Despite the Iran war, inflation and debt fears, US markets keep hitting record highs, fueled largely by AI. BBC's Samira Hussain looks into whether that bubble will burst.

items.title: Why are devastating mice plagues happening in Australia?
items.summary: Crops are being ravaged and homes are being invaded by the rodents - and it’s costing farmers hundreds of thousands of dollars.

items.title: 'Extremely intelligent' bear on the run in Japan after injuring four people
items.summary: Authorities said that a bear in north-east Japan has eluded capture by opening a window.

items.title: Watch: Guide stranded on Everest for six days rescued
items.summary: The BBC's Rajini Vaidyanathan pieces together how events unfolded.

items.title: 'It looks black' - Americans react to Reflecting Pool's completed paint job
items.summary: After weeks of renovation, water has begun refilling the historic pool, which has been coated in a colour named "American flag blue".
